steps.title: Read the y-intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept is where the graph crosses the y-axis ($x=0$). From the graph, this happens at $y=9$, giving $(0,9)$.

steps.title: Confirm the form
steps.content:
Any y-intercept must have $x=0$, ruling out $(-9,0)$ and $(-6,0)$. Of the y-axis points, only $(0,9)$ matches.

That confirms D is correct.
